notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: Assessment, AssessmentSummary, CreateAssessmentDetails, and UpdateAssessmentDetails are all polymorphic over databaseCombination MYSQL and ORACLE. The published runtime requires spec.databaseCombination explicitly and builds the matching concrete create/update body type before OCI calls.

Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateAssessment returns an Assessment body plus opc-work-request-id, while UpdateAssessment and DeleteAssessment surface only opc-work-request-id headers. The reviewed runtime therefore follows create, update, and delete with GetWorkRequest plus a concrete GetAssessment reread before projecting status or confirming delete completion.

Pinned provider-facing shape: the CRUD family is CreateAssessment, GetAssessment, ListAssessments, UpdateAssessment, and DeleteAssessment, with list filters compartmentId, displayName, lifecycleState, and lifecycleDetails plus the service-local work-request status surface from GetWorkRequest.

Repo-authored bind-versus-create stays explicit: the reviewed runtime only attempts pre-create reuse when compartmentId, displayName, and databaseCombination are all present, then narrows candidates in memory by databaseCombination and exact source/target connection identity plus network speed, downtime, database size, DDL expectation, and creation type through GetAssessment rereads.

Repo-authored mutation policy stays explicit: only the UpdateAssessmentDetails surface reconciles in place. compartmentId and databaseCombination remain replacement-only drift, and the broader object-helper fields includeObjects, excludeObjects, and bulkIncludeExcludeData are rejected because this rollout intentionally excludes the assessor/object helper families.

Repo-authored status projection and secret behavior stay explicit: the runtime projects the published Assessment status read model from concrete GetAssessment responses, preserves the shared async contract in status.async.current for work-request-backed flows, and has no Kubernetes secret side effects.
